description: CountingFive website audit skill — generates branded PDF and Markdown reports

evals.prompt: Can you run a site audit on https://www.example-cpafirm.com? We have a sales call with them next week and I want to show them what we found.
evals.expected_output: A branded PDF report and Markdown file saved to the workspace pdf/ and md/ folders. The PDF should have a cover page, executive summary with scorecard, 7 graded sections with sub-scores and narrative, and a recommendations page. Both files should follow the naming convention audit-[domain]-[date].

assertions.text: PDF file is created in the pdf/ output directory
Markdown file is created in the md/ output directory
PDF contains all 7 audit sections (mobile, speed, SEO, target market, competitive, tech stack, site health)
Each section has a letter grade (A through F scale)
Each section has numeric sub-scores in X/10 format
Report has a Recommendations section mapping findings to CountingFive services
CountingFive logo appears on the cover page

evals.prompt: Audit this site for me: https://www.quickbooksaccounting.net — I want to see how their SEO looks and whether their site is competitive.
evals.expected_output: Same output as eval 1. SEO/AIO/GEO section should be particularly detailed since the user specifically asked about SEO. Competitive Search Visibility section should show keyword ranking data.

assertions.text: SEO / AIO / GEO Health section contains sub-scores for title tags, heading structure, schema markup, and sitemap
Competitive Search Visibility section contains keyword ranking data
Narrative in each section explains findings in plain language without jargon

evals.prompt: Check this prospect's website and tell me if it's worth pursuing: https://www.greenleaftax.com
evals.expected_output: Full audit report generated. The 'worth pursuing' framing should be addressed — the recommendations section should frame findings in terms of opportunity for CountingFive (worse site = bigger opportunity). The user should get both files plus a brief in-chat summary with top 3 findings.

assertions.text: Both PDF and Markdown files are generated
In-chat response includes overall grade and top 3 findings as a brief summary
Recommendations section contains at least 3 recommendations
Each recommendation includes a CountingFive service mapping
